title: Gate POSIX-only signals and resource module to only run on POSIX Pyth…
body:
…on implementations.

The `resource` module exposes an interface to the POSIX-compliant `get/setrlimit()` functions which allow a user to set resource usage of an application. Unfortunately, this functionality isn't exposed on Windows, even on MinGW; the module doesn't exist.

This isn't great, but seeing as I need the SMT backend and I'm on a Windows machine, my current solution is to just gate out the POSIX-only functionality for now. Since this is potentially limiting on larger problems, when I have more time, I'll look into [alternate solutions](https://weblogs.asp.net/lichen/what-if-you-run-out-of-stack-space-in-c-or-python).

Similarly, `SIGHUP` isn't exposed as a signal in the `signal` module as well (nor does it make sense on Windows), so I gated this as well. I tested `SIGINT` and it works fine; AFAICT, it's [impossible](https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/12378#issuecomment-293927745) to send `SIGTERM`, but the identifier exists within `signal`, so I left it alone.
